root_text.ud8.2:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud8.2:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā bhikkhū nibbānapaṭisaṁyuttāya dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti.
root_text.ud8.2:1.4: Tedha bhikkhū aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbaṁ cetaso samannāharitvā ohitasotā dhammaṁ suṇanti.
root_text.ud8.2:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud8.2:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud8.2:1.3: Now at that time the Buddha was educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants with a Dhamma talk about extinguishment.
translation_text.ud8.2:1.4: And those mendicants were paying attention, applying the mind, concentrating wholeheartedly, and actively listening.
translation_text.ud8.2:2.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud8.2:3.1: “It’s hard to see what they call the ‘uninclined’,
translation_text.ud8.2:3.2: for the truth is not easy to see.
translation_text.ud8.2:3.3: For one who has penetrated craving,
translation_text.ud8.2:3.4: who knows and sees, there is nothing.”
